mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes designed for deployment on Ubuntu, providing a flexible and scalable solution for managing containerized applications across various cloud environments. It is ideal for enterprises looking for a robust Kubernetes setup with modern monitoring and metrics capabilities.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
First, install Juju, which is required for deploying Charmed Kubernetes. Use the following commands to install Juju on your Ubuntu system:
```bash
sudo snap install juju --classic
```

steps.title: Bootstrap Juju Controller
steps.description:
Bootstrap a Juju controller on your cloud provider. Replace `<cloud>` with your desired cloud provider (e.g., aws, gcp, azure):
```bash
juju bootstrap <cloud> my-controller
```

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes
steps.description:
Deploy Charmed Kubernetes using the following command. This command will install the latest stable bundle:
```bash
juju deploy charmed-kubernetes
```

steps.title: Verify Deployment
steps.description:
Check the status of your Kubernetes cluster to ensure all components are running:
```bash
juju status
```

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
To connect to your new Kubernetes cluster, configure kubectl:
```bash
juju config kubernetes-master kubectl-config=~/.kube/config
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up metrics and monitoring by enabling the Prometheus charm:
```bash
juju deploy prometheus
juju relate prometheus kubernetes-master
```

steps.title: Verify Cluster Info
steps.description:
Finally, verify that your cluster is operational:
```bash
kubectl cluster-info
kubectl get nodes
```

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
To uninstall Charmed Kubernetes, run the following commands to destroy the Juju controller and remove all deployed resources:
```bash
juju destroy-controller my-controller --destroy-all-models
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
To upgrade to a newer version of Charmed Kubernetes, first ensure you have a backup of your current configuration:
```bash
juju config kubernetes-master backup=true
```
Then, upgrade the bundle:
```bash
juju deploy charmed-kubernetes --revision=<new-version>
```
Replace `<new-version>` with the version you wish to upgrade to (e.g., 1.30). After the upgrade, verify the status of your deployment:
```bash
juju status
```

troubleshooting.title: Juju Controller Fails to Bootstrap
troubleshooting.description:
If the Juju controller fails to bootstrap, check the cloud credentials and network connectivity. Use:
```bash
juju bootstrap <cloud> my-controller --debug
```to get detailed logs.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes Nodes Not Ready
troubleshooting.description:
If nodes are not in a 'Ready' state, check the logs of the Kubernetes components:
```bash
juju ssh kubernetes-worker/0
sudo journalctl -u kubelet
```

troubleshooting.title: kubectl Access Issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the cluster with kubectl, ensure that the kubeconfig is correctly set up. You can regenerate it with:
```bash
juju config kubernetes-master kubectl-config=~/.kube/config
```

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus Not Collecting Metrics
troubleshooting.description:
If Prometheus is not collecting metrics, check the relation with the Kubernetes master:
```bash
juju status prometheus
```

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved metrics collection, and enhanced security features.
Added new charms for enhanced monitoring and logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Some legacy charms have been deprecated in favor of new implementations.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of Charmed Kubernetes will have all components running smoothly, with kubectl configured to access the cluster and monitoring tools operational. You should be able to deploy applications and manage the cluster effectively.
codeSnippets:
juju deploy prometheus
juju relate prometheus kubernetes-master

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (aws, gcloud, azure)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Juju and kubectl installed, and access to a cloud provider for deployment.
